verdict: 2026-07-13 10:43:26 PDT — NOT merge-ready. The server teardown and dependency shape are sound, but the reconciled documentation is still neither internally consistent nor complete: a newly published architecture asset describes the removed model, categorical logging guarantees remain, and the live backlog still instructs future work to reuse deleted storage machinery.

findings.title: Published architecture copy bypasses the canonical fix
findings.claim: The newly added public SVG still says the presenter sets each person's rate, and line 101 says names and values are encrypted and stored on the server. Its dev-docs counterpart contains the approved single-rate/session-only wording, so two copies of the same artifact have already drifted and the public one directly violates the spec.
findings.alternative: Immediately synchronize the public asset, then eliminate manual duplication: publish/copy the canonical dev-docs SVG as part of the site build or keep one source referenced by both locations.
findings.win: Removes two false public claims and centralizes the diagram invariant so subsequent edits cannot fix one copy while shipping another.

findings.title: Categorical logging guarantees still exceed `/api/log`
findings.claim: The README still calls `/api/log` PII-free, while three policies guarantee that logs never contain secrets or presenter figures. The unchanged sink serializes any submitted JSON body up to 100 KB, so these guarantees contradict both the implementation and the qualified wording already present elsewhere in the same README and Privacy Policy.
findings.alternative: Use one canonical logging statement everywhere: request-line logging plus a body-recording client sink; intended client reports are source-minimized, but the server does not enforce a PII/secret-free schema. Have secondary docs link to that statement instead of restating stronger invariants.
findings.win: Eliminates four false security claims and reduces repeated logging prose that has drifted across review rounds.

findings.title: The active backlog still plans around deleted storage machinery
findings.claim: The live tactical backlog still names the archived `roadmap.md` as authoritative and its deauthorization item says to reuse deleted `userData.purgeUser`, map identities through the removed app-context/rate-store key, and wait for the store to be enabled. This conflicts with the current canonical OPS-3 no-op-purge design and could cause the follow-up story to resurrect the architecture this branch removes.
findings.alternative: Make `BACKLOG.md` the sole current OPS-3 definition; either replace the old deauthorization section with a short pointer to OPS-3 or clearly archive its obsolete design notes, and update the roadmap reference to the archive without calling it authoritative.
findings.win: Removes one broken planning authority and prevents the marketplace follow-up from being framed against deleted modules, identity paths, and persistence.
